__fixture_tag__: VENDORED SOURCE SNAPSHOT — textbook + journal citation, frozen

claim.property: hydrolysis rate constant k_h of TEOS (tetraethyl orthosilicate) at pH 2
claim.model: acid-catalyzed sol-gel: k_h(pH 2, 25 C) ~ 1e-3 s^-1 (first-order pseudo)
claim.condition: pH 2 (HCl), 25 C, [TEOS]:[H2O] 1:4 mole ratio, ethanol solvent

source.primary: Hench & West 1990 Chem. Rev. 90, 33 — 'The Sol-Gel Process'
source.citation: Hench & West 1990 Chem. Rev. 90, 33 + Brinker & Scherer 1990 — TEOS k_h ~ 10^-3 s^-1 at pH 2, 25 C

tolerance.basis: k_h depends on water:alkoxide ratio + catalyst concentration; literature range spans ~ 1 decade
